score_components.formula_applied: Not applicable — video is instructional content, not a performance assessment

confidence_rationale: Video content is clearly educational/instructional in nature. Multiple frames show a presenter delivering competency and assessment framework instruction with animated visuals, text overlays, and classroom context footage. A single brief frame (~1-2 seconds) shows FLS Peg Transfer equipment in static setup, but no actual task performance is recorded. The dominant content is narrated instruction about educational competency frameworks, not FLS task execution.
cannot_determine: Task performance metrics (completion time, penalties) — no actual task execution is visible
Skill level of any performer — content is educational presentation, not individual assessment
Specific FLS task being taught — while Peg Transfer equipment briefly appears, the video is primarily about assessment frameworks, not task-specific instruction
classification_rationale:
This video is INSTRUCTIONAL content. It contains:
1. A presenter delivering educational material on competency assessment frameworks
2. Animated visuals and text overlays supporting the instruction
3. Classroom and institutional context footage
4. Only incidental appearance of FLS equipment (Peg Transfer trainer) without any performance demonstration

The video does NOT contain:
- An actual trainee or surgeon performing an FLS task
- Expert technique demonstration of task execution
- Timing, movement, or scoring-relevant performance data

Per the assessment instructions, instructional content with narration and educational context should NOT be scored for task performance.

verbal_cues: From tracking time to tracking competencies
Competencies as a map to learning
teaching_content.educational_focus: Competency-based assessment frameworks in educational settings; transition from time-based to competency-based evaluation; use of competencies as learning maps; ongoing formative assessment with scaffolded support

notes: This video is primarily an educational presentation about competency assessment frameworks in academic/institutional contexts. While FLS Peg Transfer equipment is briefly visible in one frame, there is no actual task performance to score. The content is instructional narration with supporting visuals, classroom documentation, and presenter explanation of assessment methodologies. No FLS task performance assessment is possible from this video.
